title: Taking Sides in Philosophy
abstract: There is a certain emotion of repugnance which I, and I hope a good many would-be philosophers, feel when asked the conventional question, “If you are a philosopher, to what school of thought do you belong? Are you an Idealist or a Realist, a Platonist or a Hobbist, a Monist or a Pluralist?”

title: The Story of American Realism
abstract: In American philosophy at the end of the nineteenth century there was small interest in Empiricism and almost no interest in Realism.
